titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Western Film Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7669329.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 12, 2018 at 19:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Western Film Collection consists of press kits, pressbooks, posters, photographs, souvenir programs and other materials that were distributed by film producers to movie theater operators to promote their films. The Western films represented in this collection are Western genre films, films set in the trans-Mississippi West, and/or films with Western characters. Press kits typically include advertisements prepared for submission to newspapers and magazines; black-and-white photographs of scenes from the films with credit information; printed booklets called pressbooks containing information about the film, actors, and other personnel; and posters for lobby display. Early press kits were called "campaign kits." While the majority of press kits included in the collection consist of foldered printed material, there are several examples of electronic press kits on compact disc. The Buddy Holly Story pressbook contains a "flexi disc" recording bound in.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 50 (sound recording): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 60 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Western Film Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Western Film Collection. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified. Further organization, rehousing, and description were carried out in 2013.

The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as UNCAT WA MS 233, UNCAT MSS 882, UNCAT MSS 1244, UNCAT MSS 1293, UNCAT MSS 1294, UNCAT MSS 1295, UNCAT MSS 1296, UNCAT MSS 1297, UNCAT MSS 1299, UNCAT MSS 1300, UNCAT MSS 1301, UNCAT MSS 1303, and UNCAT MSS 1305.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Two film titles separated in the listing by a slash mark (/) indicates that the two films were promoted together.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The Western Film Collection consists of press kits, pressbooks, posters, photographs, souvenir programs and other materials that were distributed by film producers to movie theater operators to promote their films. The Western films represented in this collection are Western genre films, films set in the trans-Mississippi West, and/or films with Western-type characters. Press kits typically include advertisements prepared for submission to newspapers and magazines; black-and-white photographs of scenes from the films with credit information; printed booklets called pressbooks containing information about the film, actors, and other personnel; and posters for lobby display. Early press kits were called campaign kits. While the majority of press kits included in the collection consist of foldered printed material, there are several examples of electronic press kits on compact disk.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in eleven groupings: I. September 1987 Acquisition, 1940-1979. II. July 2006 Acquistion, 1916-1982. III. June 2010 Acquisition, undated. IV. November 2010 Acquisition, 1932-1934. V. October 2011 Acquisition, 1958-2001. VI. January 2012 Acquisition, 1970-2007. VII. June 2012 Acquistion, 1936-1941. VIII. July 2012 Acquisition, 1936-1941. IX. September 2012 Acquisition, 1949-1950. X. December 2012 Acquisition, 1911-2005. XI. July 2013 Acquisition, 1916-1981.

processinfo.head: Processing Information note
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat WA MS 233.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Consists of press kits, pressbooks, posters, and other promotional material.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 1987.

did.unittitle: A Big Hand for the Little Lady
Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
The Castaway Cowboy / The Absent-Minded Professor
Contre les Daltons [Jesse James vs. The Daltons]
Fistful of Dollars / For a Few Dollars More
Five Bloody Graves [also known as Gun Riders]
The Girl of the Golden West
The Good Guys and The Bad Guys
The Good, The Bad, and The Ugly
Gunfight at the O.K. Corral / Last Train from Gun Hill
Hang Your Hat on the Wind
Hatari! / The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance
King Solomon's Mines / The Naked Spur
The Lawless Breed / To Hell and Back
The Life and Times of Judge Roy Bean
The Magnificent Seven / The Horse Soldiers
The Man Who Loved Cat Dancing
A Minute to Pray, a Second to Die
Once Upon a Time in the West
Pat Garrett and Billy The Kid
A Reason to Live, a Reason to Die
The Reivers / A Man Called Horse
Seven Brides for Seven Brothers / Father of the Bride
Trigger Happy [formerly known as The Deadly Companions] / The Checkered Flag

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Consists of 414 pressbooks and promotional flyers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2006.

The Lone Ranger / The Lost City of Gold
The Return of a Man Called Horse
Tell Them Willie Boy Is Here
Tex Granger Midnight Rider of the Plains
Tex Rides With the Boy Scouts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Consists of photographs, posters, lobby-cards, press and other ephemera relating to African American filmmakers of southern California.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by name of filmmaker.

did.unittitle: South Central - Press kit with five photographs
The Glass Shield - two 8" x 10" photographs
Devil in a Blue Dress - eight 8" x 10" color stills
Devil in a Blue Dress - twelve 8.5" x 11.5" German lobby cards

Dead Presidents - Press kit with four photographs
Menace II Society - nine 8" x 10" photographs
CB4 - Press kit with nine photographs
Boyz n the Hood - eight 9.5" x 13" Spanish lobby cards
Boyz n the Hood - eight 11" x 14" US lobby cards
Boyz n the Hood - twelve 8.5" x 12" German lobby cards
Boyz n the Hood - Press kit with eighteen photographs

Watermelon Man - eight 11" x 14" lobby cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes 29 flyers: 20 Mule Team (1940); Annie Get Your Gun (1951); Arizona (1931); The Barrier (1926); Beau Bandit (1930); The Big Trail (1930); Call of the West (1930); Court-Martial (1928); The Covered Wagon (1923); Debtor to the Law (1919); The Gold Rush (1925); The Golden Princess (1925); The Great Divide (1915); Laughing Boy (1934); The Love Route (1915); Mildred Pierce (1945); My Prairie Flower (1911); Mystery Ranch (1932); One Man Dog (1927); Rainbow Trail (1932); Redskin (1929); Rose-Marie (1928); San Franciso (1936); Sequoia (1934); The Spoilers (1914); Stand Up and Fight (1939); Tiger Shark (1932); Two Men of Sandy Bar (1916); What Price Hollywood (1932).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes four pressbooks: Forty Thieves (1944), Mystery Man (1944), The Devil's Playground (1946), and Hopalong Cassidy (circa 1945).

Invasion of the Body Snatchers - promotional book
It's A Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad World - promotional book
The Legend of Billie Jean - press kit
The Legend of the Lone Ranger - press kit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes ten pressbooks: Down Dakota Way (1949), The Golden Stallion (1949), Grand Canyon Trail (1948), Heart of the Rockies (1951), Song of Arizona (1946), South of Caliente (1951), Sunset in the West (1951), Sunset Serenade (1942), Susanna Pass (1949), and Twilight in the Sierras (1950).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes The Big Sleep, Bugsy, The Conversation, The Desperate Trail, Destry Rides Again, Erin Brokovich, The Godfather: Part II, The Great American Cowboy, Heaven's Gate, Hopalong Cassidy, My Prairie Flower, Pale Rider, Places in the Heart.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes The Postman Always Rings Twice, Ray, Roy Rodgers, San Antonio, Stutter's Gold, They Died with Their Boots On, Unconquered, Unforgiven, Wells Fargo.

acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund, 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a promotional flyer for Grand National Pictures 1936-37 Attractions. Grand National was a short-lived studio in operation from 1936-1939.

did.unittitle: Take a Hard Ride, press kit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a large promotional book for the film.
